failure_modes.why: RED stayed at 2 settlements for most of the game with almost no resource income, never building a real production base.
RED built only a handful of roads and never added settlements or cities, staying boxed in at 2-3 VP for hundreds of turns.
RED never upgraded any settlement to a city, forgoing the double production and VP that BLUE used to win.

review_summary: RED's engine stalled at two settlements with no expansion or city upgrades, leaving it stuck near 2-5 VP while BLUE steadily built cities and settlements to 10.

0.summary: A defensible first settlement matching the oracle's value with a useful sheep port, though resource breadth and expansion room are somewhat limited.

28.summary: A sound robber placement targeting BLUE with zero regret and an attempted steal.

failed.claude: Chose an empty tile instead of (2,-2,0) or (-2,0,2) which both touch RED, forgoing the chance to block and steal from the opponent.
failed.openai: The move doesn't affect board control but aims to hinder the opponent's resource gain.

failed.claude: Wasted the robber by placing it where it neither steals nor blocks RED's production despite RED-adjacent options being available.
failed.openai: The timing is reasonable as it aims to disrupt the opponent, though not optimal as it doesn't steal a card.

106.summary: BLUE squandered the robber on a non-producing, player-free tile when robbing RED was clearly available.

158.summary: A defensible 3:1 surplus-wheat-for-wood bank trade with no downside to the opponent, slightly inefficient on ratio.

214.summary: BLUE used a favorable 2:1 port to convert surplus wood into the brick it needed to build a road and extend its lead, a sound and constructive trade.

262.summary: A sensible port trade converting surplus wood into needed brick to continue BLUE's winning expansion.

332.summary: Sound robber placement on the runaway leader with no resource cost and zero oracle regret.

372.summary: Optimal robber placement on BLUE matching the oracle with zero regret.

422.summary: Optimal robber placement on RED's tile to steal and slow the opponent while leading.

480.summary: Strong robber placement and knight play targeting BLUE's production, matching the oracle's optimal choice.

540.summary: BLUE makes an optimal zero-cost robber placement targeting RED, fully consistent with the oracle's best move.

669.summary: A reasonable maritime trade converting surplus ORE into needed WHEAT, with only a slightly inefficient 3:1 ratio.

590.summary: RED makes the optimal robber move, targeting BLUE on a high-value tile to slow the leader.

726.summary: BLUE's knight play and robber placement on RED's tile is the oracle-optimal, cost-free choke move.

795.summary: Defensible 2:1 conversion of surplus wood from a position of strength, though wheat would have diversified the brick/wood-heavy hand better.
